def find_all(text, pattern):
    n = len(text)
    m = len(pattern)
    if m == 0:
        return []
    if m > n:
        return []
    # Convert to bytes for fast indexing
    tb = text.encode('latin-1') if isinstance(text, str) else text
    pb = pattern.encode('latin-1') if isinstance(pattern, str) else pattern
    nb = len(tb)
    mb = len(pb)
    # Boyer-Moore-Horspool with byte-level bad character table
    bad_char = [mb] * 256
    for i in range(mb - 1):
        bad_char[pb[i]] = mb - 1 - i
    results = []
    results_append = results.append
    i = 0
    while i <= nb - mb:
        j = mb - 1
        while j >= 0 and tb[i + j] == pb[j]:
            j -= 1
        if j < 0:
            results_append(i)
            i += 1
        else:
            i += bad_char[tb[i + mb - 1]]
    return results

def find_all(text, pattern):
    n = len(text)
    m = len(pattern)
    if m == 0:
        return []
    if m > n:
        return []
    if m == 1:
        c = pattern[0]
        return [i for i in range(n) if text[i] == c]
    # Convert to bytes for fast element access
    tb = text.encode('latin-1') if isinstance(text, str) else text
    pb = pattern.encode('latin-1') if isinstance(pattern, str) else pattern
    nb = len(tb)
    mb = len(pb)
    # Boyer-Moore-Horspool
    bad_char = [mb] * 256
    for i in range(mb - 1):
        bad_char[pb[i]] = mb - 1 - i
    results = []
    append = results.append
    last = pb[mb - 1]
    i = 0
    nm = nb - mb
    while i <= nm:
        # Check last character first for early bail-out
        if tb[i + mb - 1] == last:
            j = mb - 2
            while j >= 0 and tb[i + j] == pb[j]:
                j -= 1
            if j < 0:
                append(i)
                i += 1
                continue
        i += bad_char[tb[i + mb - 1]]
    return results

def find_all(text, pattern):
    n = len(text)
    m = len(pattern)
    if m == 0:
        return []
    if m > n:
        return []
    if m == 1:
        c = pattern[0]
        return [i for i in range(n) if text[i] == c]
    # Convert to bytes
    tb = text.encode('latin-1') if isinstance(text, str) else text
    pb = pattern.encode('latin-1') if isinstance(pattern, str) else pattern
    nb = len(tb)
    mb = len(pb)
    # Boyer-Moore-Horspool with Sunday's improvement
    # Shift table looks at the character AFTER the current window
    bad_char = [mb + 1] * 256
    for i in range(mb):
        bad_char[pb[i]] = mb - i
    results = []
    append = results.append
    i = 0
    nm = nb - mb
    while i <= nm:
        j = 0
        while j < mb and tb[i + j] == pb[j]:
            j += 1
        if j == mb:
            append(i)
        if i + mb < nb:
            i += bad_char[tb[i + mb]]
        else:
            break
    return results

def find_all(text, pattern):
    n = len(text)
    m = len(pattern)
    if m == 0:
        return []
    if m > n:
        return []
    if m == 1:
        c = pattern[0]
        return [i for i in range(n) if text[i] == c]
    # Convert to bytes for fast byte-level operations
    tb = text.encode('latin-1') if isinstance(text, str) else text
    pb = pattern.encode('latin-1') if isinstance(pattern, str) else pattern
    nb = len(tb)
    mb = len(pb)
    # Boyer-Moore-Horspool with combined shift tables
    # Bad character shift based on last position
    bad_char = [mb] * 256
    for i in range(mb - 1):
        bad_char[pb[i]] = mb - 1 - i
    results = []
    append = results.append
    i = 0
    nm = nb - mb
    last_pb = pb[mb - 1]
    first_pb = pb[0]
    while i <= nm:
        # Quick pre-checks on first and last chars before full comparison
        if tb[i + mb - 1] == last_pb and tb[i] == first_pb:
            if tb[i:i + mb] == pb:
                append(i)
                i += 1
                continue
        i += bad_char[tb[i + mb - 1]]
    return results
